title: Serapias spiralis
intro: Serapias spiralis is a species of orchid native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the genus Serapias, characterized by its spiral or curved shape. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Serapias spiralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Serapias spiralis is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorant remedies.
